name: Accounts Payable
tagline: The payables cycle proved rather than assumed
plain_summary: Accounts payable is where controls are asserted most often and proven least often. This engine reads the fixed-format posting artifacts the cycle already emits and runs thirty deterministic controls over them: did the post actually happen and balance, is this payment allowed to be released yet, is every job routed to a valid approver chain with duties segregated, and will year-end information reporting be complete. It never posts, never pays, and never writes to a source artifact. Every exception it raises ends at a person.
marketing_thesis: A control engine is only credible if it can be wrong in public. This one ships the defects it claims to catch: one planted defect per registered control, each of which must trip its own control and leave the other twenty-nine silent. The benchmark is a command, not a claim.
layers_intro: Six control families run in registry order over each document set. The first proves the others had something to read.

layers.label: Structural Precondition
layers.plain: Checks the document set actually carries every artifact the controls below need to read.
layers.engineering: check_set_complete compares the doc_type values present against model.DOC_TYPES and FAILs when any of the five are absent. Registered first because every other rule iterates one artifact type and returns nothing when it is missing -- absent evidence would otherwise read as a held control and the set would be filed as PASS.

layers.label: Posting Integrity
layers.plain: Confirms the posting run happened, balanced, and was not aborted.
layers.engineering: Eight controls over the invoice posting report: proof figure exactly zero, ledger recap debits equal credits, posting totals balanced, zero rejects, a non-zero posted count, no blocking error marker, job-cost recap tied to the payable cost total, and header date agreeing with the file-name stamp.

layers.label: Payment Release Gate
layers.plain: Checks every compliance gate is closed before a payment is allowed out.
layers.engineering: Eight controls over the payment selection register: taxpayer certificate on file before a first payment, waivers from the subcontractor and its lower tiers, insurance current at the payment date and meeting contract minimums, funding confirmed, no duplicate obligation in the run, retention and tax lines present and rate-consistent, and off-cycle payments carrying a recorded approver.

layers.label: Routing and Duties
layers.plain: Checks every job reaches a real approver chain and that nobody approves their own work.
layers.engineering: Five controls over the workflow routing matrix: every active job maps to exactly one workflow, every workflow has an approver and a named final-review group, data-entry and final-review roles are held by different people, and direct-post workflows are explicitly enumerated.

layers.label: Information Reporting
layers.plain: Checks year-end vendor reporting will be complete and correctly identified.
layers.engineering: Five controls over the information reporting register: every vendor over the reporting threshold is evaluated, each reportable vendor carries a structurally valid taxpayer identification number, no two vendor records share one identifier, and filed-form counts reconcile to the expected list per entity.

layers.label: Commitment Hygiene
layers.plain: Checks commitments mirror their contracts and change orders attach where they belong.
layers.engineering: Three controls over the commitment register: the schedule of values mirrors the contract rather than collapsing to a single lump line, commitment identifiers follow the project and vendor convention, and every change order attaches to a commitment that exists in the register.

blocks.label: Seeded Document Sets
blocks.plain: fictional posting document sets enter the control registry
blocks.engineering: generate_corpus writes one clean baseline plus one planted defect per registered control from a single seeded Random instance

blocks.label: Structural Precondition
blocks.plain: proves the controls below had something to read
blocks.engineering: check_set_complete -- FAILs when any of the five artifact types is absent

blocks.label: Posting Integrity
blocks.plain: did the post happen, and did it balance
blocks.engineering: eight controls: proof, ledger recap, posting totals, rejects, posted count, error markers, job-cost tie, header date

blocks.label: Payment Release Gate
blocks.plain: is this payment allowed out yet
blocks.engineering: eight controls: taxpayer certificate, waivers, insurance currency and limits, funding, duplicates, retention, off-cycle approval

blocks.label: Routing and Duties
blocks.plain: does every job reach a real approver, with duties split
blocks.engineering: five controls: job mapping, approver presence, final-review group, duty segregation, declared direct-post

blocks.label: Information Reporting
blocks.engineering: five controls: threshold coverage, identifier presence and structure, split-vendor detection, filed-count reconciliation

blocks.label: Commitment Hygiene
blocks.engineering: three controls: schedule of values not a lump line, identifier convention, change-order attachment

blocks.label: Findings
blocks.plain: each control yields a finding carrying its own rationale
blocks.engineering: frozen Finding(rule, status, location, message); location is doc_type:document_id/field path and the message states why the control exists

blocks.label: Verdict
blocks.plain: one verdict per document set, worst finding wins
blocks.engineering: any FAIL rolls up to FAIL; otherwise any FLAG rolls up to REVIEW; otherwise PASS. The process exit code carries the same verdict.

blocks.label: Human Gate
blocks.plain: a person decides; the engine has no write path
blocks.engineering: the engine emits markdown and JSON only. It holds no connection to a ledger, payment provider or approval system, so nothing it produces can post or pay.

spec_strip.source: python -m pytest --collect-only -q -o addopts= (accounts-payable-automation/); 2,223 tests collected on 2026-07-21 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
spec_strip.plain: the collected checks that pin this engine's behavior

spec_strip.label: Controls
spec_strip.source: accounts-payable-automation/ap_engine/engine.py REGISTRY; mirrored in the rule-registry table of accounts-payable-automation/README.md
spec_strip.plain: independent payables controls, run in registry order

spec_strip.label: Families
spec_strip.plain: groups the controls fall into, from structural to commitment hygiene

spec_strip.label: Planted defects
spec_strip.source: accounts-payable-automation/ap_engine/generate.py DEFECTS; test_checks_coverage.py asserts the defect set equals the registry
spec_strip.plain: one deliberate fault per control, so none can silently stop working

spec_strip.label: Artifact types
spec_strip.plain: the source documents a payables cycle emits

spec_strip.label: Write paths
spec_strip.source: accounts-payable-automation/ap_engine/tests/test_readonly_and_determinism.py -- source artifacts are hashed before and after a run and asserted byte-identical
spec_strip.plain: the engine reports; it never posts, pays, or edits a source

problem_statement: Three payables failures recur across the industry and all three are invisible to the people running the cycle. A posting run aborts on batch contention and is filed as though it succeeded -- the report still balances, because nothing moved. A payment is released through an open compliance gate: no taxpayer certificate on a first payment, waivers missing from a subcontractor's lower tiers, insurance expired before the payment date. And a routing matrix drifts until a job maps to no workflow, or one person holds both data entry and final review. Each is deterministic to detect from artifacts that already exist, and none of them needs a model's judgment.

scenarios.title: The seeded demo runs every control over every document set
scenarios.narrative: The CLI generates thirty-one fictional document sets -- one clean baseline plus one planted defect per control -- and runs all thirty controls over each. The clean set reports PASS with every control held; each defective set reports the exact control it was built to trip. The run exits 2 because the corpus deliberately contains failures.
scenarios.source: python run.py (accounts-payable-automation/); output re-run on 2026-07-21; committed as accounts-payable-automation/ap_report.md

scenarios.title: Absent evidence must not read as a passing control
scenarios.narrative: One fixture removes an entire artifact type from its document set. Every control that reads that artifact returns nothing, which without a structural precondition would render as a clean result. The set_complete control is registered first and fails the set, so the report opens with proof that the controls below it had something to read.
scenarios.source: accounts-payable-automation/ap_engine/engine.py check_set_complete; fixture in generate.py _defect_set_incomplete; asserted in ap_engine/tests/test_generate_and_cli_coverage.py

scenarios.title: Every control must prove both of its sides
scenarios.narrative: Each of the thirty controls is exercised against its own planted defect, which it must catch, and against the clean baseline, where it must stay silent. A coverage test asserts the planted-defect set and the registry are the same set, so a control cannot be added without a fixture that exercises it, and an implementation that always passes or always fails cannot satisfy the suite.

instruction_set.operation: generate the seeded fictional corpus, run all thirty controls, write both artifacts
instruction_set.output: per-document-set verdicts and every actionable finding, then the overall verdict
instruction_set.exit_code: 2 for the bundled corpus, which carries one planted defect per control

instruction_set.operation: analyze an existing folder of document sets, read-only
instruction_set.exit_code: 0 PASS, 1 REVIEW, 2 FAIL, 3 usage or IO error

instruction_set.operation: print only the overall verdict line
instruction_set.exit_code: 0 PASS, 1 REVIEW, 2 FAIL, 3 usage or IO error

instruction_set.operation: run the curated suite exactly as CI does

benchmarks.label: Engine tests
benchmarks.source: python -m pytest --collect-only -q -o addopts= (accounts-payable-automation/); re-run 2026-07-21 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
benchmarks.plain: live collection from the engine directory

benchmarks.label: Curated invariant grid
benchmarks.plain: bounded arithmetic and verdict identities, asserted exactly over integers

benchmarks.label: Planted-defect cases
benchmarks.plain: every control against its own defect and against a clean baseline

benchmarks.label: Opt-in property sweep
benchmarks.plain: the exhaustive grid, gated behind SWEEP=1 so it never slows CI

control_characteristics.plain: A clean result means the implemented mechanical controls found nothing. It is not sign-off, and it is not authorization to pay. Release, approval and posting all stay outside the engine.

verdict_map.action: send the mechanically clean result to the documented manual gates

verdict_map.severity: One or more FLAG, no FAIL
verdict_map.action: route the flagged items to a person for judgment; do not treat as cleared
hold the affected posting or payment and resolve at source before re-running

guarantees: Money is integer cents everywhere and is compared with exact equality; there is no tolerance band anywhere in this engine, and a non-integer amount is rejected rather than coerced.
The engine is read-only: source artifacts are hashed before and after a run and asserted byte-identical, so it is structurally incapable of introducing the defects it looks for.
One planted defect exists per registered control, and a coverage test asserts the two sets are identical, so a control cannot be added without a fixture that exercises it.
The structural precondition control runs first, so a document set missing an artifact type cannot report as clean simply because the controls that read it never ran.
The package declares no runtime dependencies; the optional workbook rendering is behind a guarded import and never byte-compared.

gate_policy.demo_gate: FAIL on the bundled corpus, by design -- it carries one planted defect per control
gate_policy.note: The engine has no write path and therefore no autonomy to gate. A verdict is an input to a person's decision, never a substitute for it: posting, payment release and approval all remain outside the engine, and no finding it raises is actioned without review.

limits.statement: The engine never posts, releases a payment, drafts a journal entry, creates an import payload, or mutates a source system. It has no connection to a ledger, payment provider or approval system.
A PASS verdict means the implemented mechanical controls found nothing; it is not sign-off, audit assurance, or authorization to pay.

The engine reads structured document sets, not scanned images. There is no optical character recognition, and an artifact with no machine-readable content is reported as unclassifiable rather than guessed at.
There is no remediation loop. Resyncing to authority and booking adjustments is a mutation, which a read-only control engine must not perform; this engine is a sensor other loops consume.
limits.source: specs/2026-07-21-ap-engine-design.md -- Architecture; accounts-payable-automation/README.md -- What this demonstrates

limits.statement: All shipped data is fictional and seeded. The engine names no commercial software product and carries no real vendor, entity, person, or document identifier.

media.poster_alt: Accounts Payable diagram: a posting report and a ledger recap joined by a green verified check reading debits equals credits and proof zero, above four payment release gates for taxpayer certificate, lien waivers, insurance and funding, each ticked green
media.caption: The Accounts Payable engine tile depicts the posting tie-out above the four payment release gates, with the note that any unmet gate holds the payment for a person.

meta.description: Accounts Payable (SFS-E10-APX) datasheet -- thirty deterministic, read-only controls over the payables cycle: posting integrity, the payment release gate, approver routing with duties segregated, information reporting, and commitment hygiene, all ending at an explicit human-review boundary.
